stories.title: Pentagon orders troops from 82nd Airborne Division to deploy to Middle East
stories.description: Nearly a month into the war with Iran, the Trump administration is keeping its options open: it says it is pursuing diplomatic solutions with Iran, while ordering thousands of paratroopers to deploy in the Middle East.

stories.title: Rubio plans travel to France to sell Iran war to skeptical G7 allies
stories.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to France this week to try to sell America's skeptical Group of Seven allies on the Iran war that has sent global fuel prices soaring.

stories.title: The Israeli military wants several more weeks to fight Iran war, officials say
stories.description: The Israeli military estimates it would need several more weeks of fighting to complete its war goals in Iran, at a time when President Trump says the U.S. is negotiating an end to the war.

stories.title: Senate confirms Sen. Mullin as DHS secretary. And, Iran denies U.S. talks to end war
stories.description: The Senate has confirmed Markwayne Mullin as the next Department of Homeland Security secretary. And, Iran has denied that it's in talks with the U.S. to end the war, which is now in its fourth week.

stories.title: Trump declares victory and claims Iran offers a 'prize' in talks Iran has denied having
stories.description: President Trump said on Tuesday Iran made a valuable offer to reach a deal with the United States, in talks that Iran has denied are taking place. "We've won this. This war has been won," Trump said.

stories.title: Asia boosts coal use as Iran war squeezes global LNG supplies
stories.description: Analysts say coal may stabilize supplies for now but they warn that continued reliance on the polluting fuel will worsen air pollution.

stories.title: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger – the Iranian civilians killed in the war
stories.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

stories.title: US' and Iran's options for ending war narrow the longer it goes on
stories.description: Iran shows no sign of climbdown, while air power alone cannot achieve the US' main goal.

stories.title: Israel to expand ground and air attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.description: There are fears in Lebanon that Israel is preparing for a large-scale ground invasion.

stories.title: Iranian missiles injure 180 in towns near Israeli nuclear site
stories.description: Israel is investigating how ballistic missiles got through the country's sophisticated air defences.

stories.title: Trump at a crossroads as US weighs tough options in Iran
stories.description: Statements from President Trump that the war is almost over are not matching up with the reality on the ground.

stories.title: Why there's no sign of the conflict in the Middle East 'winding down'
stories.description: Iran's foreign minister said that it doesn't appear that the US is prepared to halt its aggression after President Trump said the US was considering "winding down" its war.

stories.title: US lifts sanctions on some Iranian oil as energy prices soar
stories.description: The treasury secretary says the move will quickly bring about 140m barrels of oil to global markets.

stories.title: Iranian strikes on bases used by US caused $800m in damage, new analysis shows
stories.description: Much of the damage was caused in initial retaliatory strikes by Iran in the week after the US and Israel launched its operation.

stories.title: UK allows US to use bases to strike Strait of Hormuz targets
stories.description: Downing Street says ministers approved the expansion after accusing Iran of "reckless strikes".

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: 'It's been bittersweet': The Iranian-Iraqi cafe drawing crowds to Hackney
stories.description: Logma, a newly opened Iranian-Iraqi cafe in Hackney, has gone viral for its sandwiches.

stories.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
stories.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

stories.title: How Iranians are evading internet blocks to contact family abroad
stories.description: Iranians are finding tech-savvy ways to get through the regime's restrictions on phone and internet connections.

stories.title: Under drone fire, exiled Kurds wait to confront Iranian regime
stories.description: A group of fighters based in Iraq say they are "ready to go home", as they face drone attacks from Tehran.

stories.title: As hopes of regime change in Iran fade, Netanyahu faces political test
stories.description: Israeli leaders are framing the bombing campaign as having transformed the Middle East in their favour as pressure to end the conflict builds.

stories.title: 'Mind-blowing corruption': Alleged insider trading over US-Israeli strikes in Iran
stories.description: PRESS REVIEW – Wednesday, March 25: Papers discuss Donald Trump's 15-point plan and the US's "fog of diplomacy". Could it be staged for the global markets? Also: brick workers are coerced into selling their kidneys in Pakistan. Finally, Democrat Emily Gregory wins the hotly conte...

stories.title: US ramps up military presence in the Middle East amid reported talks
stories.description: The United States is ramping up its military presence in the Middle East even more, despite the US having reportedly sent a 15-point peace plan to Tehran. There are plans to send soldiers from the 82nd Airborne Division to support operations against Iran, according to US media re...

stories.title: War in the Middle East: An opportunity for Iran's minorities?
stories.description: Shiite Persians make up 51 percent of Iran's population. Yet communities like the Azeris, Kurds, Balochs and Arabs also number in the millions. Historically, they have often been ignored or mistreated by the regime in Tehran, both under the shah and the Islamic Republic. These et...

stories.title: US sends 15-point peace plan to Iran while deploying troops in the region
stories.description: The Trump administration is ramping up efforts to end the Middle East war. After Trump said talks were ongoing between Washington and Tehran, Pakistani officials said that the White House had sent a 15-point peace plan to Iran - yet Tehran has dismissed the idea of negotiating wi...

stories.title: Middle East live: Israeli strikes on South Lebanon leave nine dead
stories.description: In Lebanon, Israeli strikes killed at least six people in a town and a Palestinian refugee camp in the southern Sidon area, and three more in another town. This comes as US President Donald Trump expresses optimism at ending the war, after sending a 15-point ceasefire plan to Ira...

stories.title: US proposes 15-point plan as Iran opens Strait of Hormuz to 'non-hostile' oil vessels
stories.description: US President Donald Trump said Tuesday he had sent a peace plan to Iran, expressing cautious optimism about ending nearly a month of war. In a potential sign of de-escalation, Tehran announced it would allow “non-hostile” vessels to pass through the strategically vital Strait of ...

stories.title: Spain’s PM says Israel to inflict on Lebanon same destruction as in Gaza
stories.description: Pedro ​Sanchez has been one of the few leaders in Europe to condemn Israel's war in Lebanon, US-Israeli ⁠war on Iran.

stories.title: UN human rights chief warns war on Iran is spreading
stories.description: UN human rights chief Volker Turk says the war on Iran is spreading across the region, with civilians bearing the brunt.

stories.title: Iran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz is an international crisis
stories.description: As such, it requires urgent action.

stories.title: ‘A heinous crime’: Air strikes kill seven fighters in Iraq’s Anbar
stories.description: Police source tells Al Jazeera attack hits positions of the Iran-aligned PMF, which the US has increasingly targeted.

stories.title: US Democrats condemn war on Iran as Senate resolution fails
stories.description: The latest war powers resolution in the US Senate to end US attacks on Iran has failed in a 47-53 vote.

stories.title: Is Iran’s negotiating position stronger than when US-Israeli war started?
stories.description: Tehran believes so, and will seek to extract concessions that the US and Gulf nations can't agree to, analysts caution.

stories.title: US talking to itself, says Iran as Trump claims wheels of diplomacy turning
stories.description: Iranian officials continue to deny any peace talks, amid reports the US sent the country a ceasefire proposal.

stories.title: ‘Do not call your defeat an agreement’ IRGC warns US, Israel
stories.description: The spokesman for the IRGC has rejected President Trump’s suggestions that talks have been taking place with Iran.

stories.title: Iran’s new security boss Mohammad Zolghadr: Why his appointment matters
stories.description: Zolghadr, an ex-IRGC commander, will manage Iran's security amid external US-Israeli pressures and internal unrest.

stories.title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 26 of attacks?
stories.description: Iran, Israel, the Gulf faced new missiles and drones. The US has signalled military escalation and openness to a deal.

stories.title: Amid US-Israeli attacks, people in Iran struggle to survive ailing economy
stories.description: Some are left without an income, others are temporarily displaced, and no one knows when the internet will return .

stories.title: From Pakistan to Egypt, Iran war drives up prices in Global South
stories.description: Developing economies in Asia, Africa and the Middle East are most exposed to surging energy costs.

stories.title: Parsi: No deal ‘’without both sides giving something to the other’
stories.description: Trita Parsi argues that Iran is unlikely to agree to end the war without sanctions relief.

stories.title: New Zealand footballers willing to play Iran World Cup match outside US
stories.description: The teams are set to play on June 15 in Los Angeles, but Iran has threatened to boycott its matches due to the war.

stories.title: What we know about the US’s 15-point plan Iran proposal
stories.description: US media reports the Trump administration has proposed a temporary ceasefire and a 15-point plan to end the war on Iran.

stories.title: US reportedly sends peace plan as Iran opens Strait of Hormuz to ‘non-hostile’ oil vessels
stories.description: Washington sent a peace plan to Iran, US media reported, as Donald Trump voiced optimism Tuesday at ending nearly a month of warfare and Tehran announced that it will let “non-hostile” oil vessels go through the crucial Strait of Hormuz. The tentative signs of a diplomatic soluti...

stories.title: PM Shehbaz hails Saudi Arabia’s ‘restraint’, stresses urgent need for de-escalation of ongoing Iran war
stories.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Wednesday hailed Saudi Arabia for showing “remarkable restraint” during the ongoing Middle East hostilities and stressed the need for urgent de-escalation. He made the remarks during a phone call with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, wherei...

stories.title: Pakistan’s mediation offer reflects growing diplomatic confidence, Capitol Hill symposium told
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Speakers at an hours-long symposium on Capitol Hill on Tuesday noted that Pakistan’s gesture to mediate peace between the United States and Iran, amid the recent escalation of hostilities between Washington and Tehran, reflects growing confidence in Islamabad’s diplom...

stories.title: Pakistan joins UNSC in denouncing illegal Israeli settlements
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Pakistan on Tuesday joined seven other members of the UN Security Council in reiterating firm opposition to the annexation of any part of the Occupied Palestinian Territory and the forcible displacement of the Palestinian people. All seven nations warned that such act...

stories.title: AJK PM condemns sentencing of Kashmiri leader Asiya Andrabi, her associates by Indian court
stories.description: MUZAFFARABAD: Azad Jammu and Kashmir (AJK) Prime Minister Raja Faisal Mumtaz Rathore on Tuesday strongly condemned the sentencing of three women activists by an Indian court, terming the verdict a manifestation of “political coercion” aimed at silencing dissent in India-occupied ...

stories.title: US-Israel war on Iran may increase food prices worldwide: UN
stories.description: ISLAMABAD: The United Nations Food and Agriculture Organisation (FAO) has said that the prolonged disruption to trade through the Strait of Hormuz triggered by US/Israeli attack on Iran could create cascading impacts across energy markets, fertiliser supply chains and global agri...

stories.title: Freak storms to hit war-ravaged Middle East
stories.description: A TORNADO Alley-style storm system is about to hit the Middle East — a virtually unheard of atmospheric setup for the region, the Washington Post reported, as areas in the Gulf region received heavy rains. Parts of the Arabian Peninsula and Persian Gulf could be slammed by strong...

stories.title: US mulls sending elite airborne troops to Middle East
stories.description: • Energy infrastructure attacked as Israel claims ‘large wave of strikes’ on Isfahan, other areas• Iran says projectile hit its Bushehr nuclear power plant• Tel Aviv, Ben Gurion airport struck by drones• 498 schools, 281 medical facilities among over 82,000 civilian structures ta...

stories.title: North Korea’s Kim vows ‘irreversible’ nuclear status, warns Seoul of ‘merciless’ response
stories.description: North Korea will never give up nuclear weapons, leader Kim Jong Un said, indicating that it will soon designate South Korea the “most hostile state”, according to a state media report on Tuesday. Kim also told the country’s legislature in a policy address on Monday that the Unite...

stories.title: Islamabad offers to host US-Iran climbdown effort
stories.description: • Insiders say Washington asked Pakistan to host negotiations; groundwork laid in weeks of ‘hectic diplomacy’• Witkoff expected to lead engagement; no indication yet of who Iran may send• Trump claims engagement on ‘15 points’• Tehran implies existence of backchannel; spox says m...

stories.title: Australia, European Union agree sweeping new trade pact 8 years in the works
stories.description: The European Union (EU) and Australia struck a long-awaited free-trade deal on Tuesday, while also agreeing to boost defence cooperation and access to crucial rare-earth minerals in the face of global uncertainty over trade. EU chief Ursula von der Leyen’s visit to Australia come...

stories.title: US journalists sue to protect Voice of America from political control
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Journalists at the US government-funded broadcaster Voice of America (VOA) have filed a lawsuit accusing the Trump administration of undermining the outlet’s independence and turning it into a tool for political messaging. The complaint, filed on Monday in the US Dist...

stories.title: War Diary Day 25: Pause holds but conflict’s underlying dynamics point to widening of scope
stories.description: On the 25th day of the US-Israeli war on Iran, the conflict’s underlying dynamics pointed to a continued widening of scope, with President Donald Trump’s five-day pause on strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure offering only limited respite and doing little to alter the br...

stories.title: US deploys ICE agents at 14 airports
stories.description: WASHINGTON: The US immigration agency at the centre of a firestorm over heavy-handed enforcement tactics began deploying to major airports on Monday, as officials scrambled to ease mounting travel disruption during a prolonged, partial government shutdown. The move places Immigra...

stories.title: Pakistan in the limelight as US, Iran ‘open backchannel’
stories.description: • Efforts alongside Egypt and Turkiye seen as key to pausing threatened US strikes on Iran’s energy infrastructure• Administration sources say diplomatic efforts saw Islamabad leveraging its position as ‘net regional stabiliser’• PM speaks to Pezeshkian, Dar calls Araghchi; White...

stories.title: Iran’s uranium enrichment secures independence, peaceful atom — supreme leader
stories.description: Ali Khamenei added that the US and other countries are not interested in Iran's progress and have put forward conditions presupposing a complete halt of uranium enrichment on Iranian territory

stories.title: Tehran could weigh nuclear deal with US with Iran-based enrichment consortium — Axios
stories.description: According to the report, a sixth round of talks on the Iranian nuclear program between the US president’s Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iran’s top diplomat, Abbas Araghchi, could be held this weekend in a Middle Eastern country

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
